nodes.description: Low sodium intake enhances ENaC blocker efficacy by reducing sodium substrate load at the channel pore.

evidence.reference_title: Liddle Syndrome: Review of the Literature and Description of a New Case.
evidence.snippet: According to the pathophysiology, the efficacy of the ENaC blockers is enhanced by dietary low salt intake

nodes.description: Amiloride or triamterene directly inhibit epithelial sodium channels, reducing sodium retention and improving blood pressure and hypokalemia.

evidence.reference_title: Liddle Syndrome: Review of the Literature and Description of a New Case.
evidence.snippet: The specific treatment of LS is represented by K+-sparing diuretics amiloride and triamterene, that are ENaC blockers.

evidence.reference_title: Liddle Syndrome: Review of the Literature and Description of a New Case.
evidence.snippet: ENaC blockers are effective in normalizing both blood pressure and the typical biochemical alterations

nodes.description: Gain-of-function variants in SCNN1A, SCNN1B, or SCNN1G increase activity or membrane abundance of the epithelial sodium channel in distal nephron epithelial cells.
nodes.id: ENaC gain of function in distal nephron

evidence.reference_title: Liddle Syndrome: Review of the Literature and Description of a New Case.
evidence.snippet: Liddle syndrome results from germline mutations in SCNN1A, SCNN1B or SCNN1G genes.

evidence.reference_title: Liddle Syndrome: Review of the Literature and Description of a New Case.
evidence.snippet: The mutations are in fact responsible for an augmented apical membrane channel density and a subsequent increase in amiloride-sensitive inward sodium current

nodes.description: Chronic systemic arterial hypertension is the central clinical manifestation of sodium retention and volume expansion.

evidence.reference_title: Liddle syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0000822 | Hypertension | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Liddle Syndrome: Review of the Literature and Description of a New Case.
evidence.snippet: Systematic review of the reported cases revealed that hypertension is present in 92.4% of the patients

nodes.description: Low circulating potassium results from increased distal renal potassium secretion downstream of ENaC-mediated sodium reabsorption.

evidence.reference_title: Liddle syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0002900 | Hypokalemia | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Liddle Syndrome: Review of the Literature and Description of a New Case.
evidence.snippet: hypokalemia (defined as serum K+ <3.5 mmol/L) in 71.8%

nodes.description: Metabolic alkalosis accompanies potassium wasting in the distal nephron and is part of the classic biochemical pattern.

evidence.reference_title: Liddle Syndrome: Review of the Literature and Description of a New Case.
evidence.snippet: Biochemically, the characteristic findings are hypokalemia, metabolic alkalosis, suppressed PRA (plasma renin activity) and low serum aldosterone levels

nodes.description: Many beta- and gamma-subunit variants truncate or disrupt the C-terminal PY motif, impairing Nedd4-mediated ENaC internalization and proteasomal degradation, which increases apical membrane channel density.

evidence.reference_title: Liddle Syndrome: Review of the Literature and Description of a New Case.
evidence.snippet: The loss of the proline-rich sequence prevents the internalization and degradation of the channel via the ubiquitination-proteasomal pathway

nodes.description: Increased distal sodium entry through ENaC favors renal potassium secretion through apical potassium channels, producing renal potassium wasting and hypokalemia.

evidence.reference_title: Liddle Syndrome: Review of the Literature and Description of a New Case.
evidence.snippet: which exit the apical membrane through different K+ channels and are lost in the urine (resulting in hypokalemia and metabolic alkalosis)

nodes.description: Increased ENaC-mediated sodium reabsorption causes extracellular volume expansion and suppresses renin and aldosterone while producing salt-sensitive hypertension.
nodes.id: Renal sodium retention and volume expansion

evidence.reference_title: Liddle Syndrome: Review of the Literature and Description of a New Case.
evidence.snippet: Hypertension results from increased Na+ reabsorption at the distal nephron level, leading to volume expansion

evidence.reference_title: Liddle Syndrome: Review of the Literature and Description of a New Case.
evidence.snippet: responsible for the observed biochemical phenotype of low renin and low serum aldosterone
